title: notifications core — authorization policies
description: Three Eloquent policies wire abilities onto the core models. Every policy inherits foundation's `HasSystemFlag` refusal rule for is_system=true rows. Channel modules ship their own policies for their side-state models (MailSuppression, PushSubscription, SmsOptOut).

abilities.scope: auth user is the addressee OR has platform.notifications.viewAny
auth user is the addressee OR has platform.notifications.view. Anonymous notifications (addressee_type=anonymous) require platform.notifications.view.
REFUSED for regular users. Only platform admins can hard-delete (typically only via ExpungeArchivedNotificationsJob after retention).
platform admins only. Requires an existing failed delivery. Rate-limited to prevent replay abuse.
template tenant_id === auth tenant OR is_system=true OR platform admin
tenant admin (creates a tenant-override of a platform template)
tenant admin AND (template.tenant_id === auth tenant OR is_system=false). PLATFORM templates (tenant_id=null, is_system=true) are edit-refused for non-super_admin per HasSystemFlag.
REFUSED when live sends reference the row (see NotificationTemplateObserver@deleting)
tenant admin AND draft-→published transition requires QA-signed metadata (SOC 2 CC8.1 change management)
author of the draft OR tenant admin. Rate-limited to 10/day per template.
auth user views only own preferences
preference.user_id === auth user. Bulk update: every row must belong to auth user.
auth user OR cascade-purge listener on UserErased. Manual delete rare — preferences update in place.
tenant admin views ANY user's preferences in tenant. Used for support diagnostics + DPO Art. 30 records-of-processing.

notes: NotificationCategory has NO policy — it's a system registry, seeded, read-only for regular users. Platform admins manage via seeder + `notifications:seed-categories` command.
NotificationDelivery has NO policy — it's a subordinate to Notification. Access derives from parent Notification's viewability.
NotificationDigest has NO policy — it's transient batching state, not user-facing directly (users see the resulting digest as a Notification row).
